title: Understanding the Problem
text: To find the velocity of a person hitting the ground, we need to consider the gravitational acceleration and the height from which the person falls.

title: Gravitational Acceleration
text: The acceleration due to gravity is approximately $$9.8$$ m/s². This value is crucial for calculating the velocity of a falling object.

title: Using the Kinematic Equation
text: The kinematic equation to find the final velocity of a falling object is $$v=\sqrt{2g h}$$, where g is the acceleration due to gravity and $$h$$ is the height.

title: Calculate the Velocity
text: Substitute the values into the equation $$v=\sqrt{2\times9.8\times5}$$.
